text: Alright I started the working doc for the Feb 5 review. Confluence page PG-013. Mike I put a rough agenda outline in there - four corridors plus tablet issues plus policy updates. Let me know if I'm missing anything.
Looks like a solid start Anthony. Make sure we have incident trend data going back to at least Q2 2026 so Sandra can see the full picture. She'll want numbers not just anecdotes.
I can pull the safety bulletin history for all four corridors. Also want to flag that SB-2026-014 is still linked in the HOS guide - need to update that reference to SB-2026-022 before the meeting.

Good catch Rosa. I filed SAFE-204 for the stale bulletin reference. Kevin can you get me the fleet stop utilization numbers for Oct Nov Dec? I need driver counts and incident tags for all four corridor stops.
On it. I'll have the extract by end of week. FYI the Fleet Stop Log shows a spike in security tags at a couple independent lots on I-20 in November. Might want to dig into that.

Heads up all drivers - new safety bulletin SB-2027-001 posted for I-20 westbound construction zone near Oxford AL mile markers 118-124. Reduced shoulder width through March. Slow it down through there.
Copy that Rosa. I drove through there last week - it's tight. Adding a note to the I-20 route record in Airtable.

My tablet did the cold-start reboot thing again this morning in Chattanooga. 22 degrees outside. Had to pull the battery and let it warm up in the cab for 10 minutes before it would boot. This is the third time since the v4.2 update.
Sorry about that Anthony. We're seeing continued reports from northern routes. Dani is working on a v4.2.1 hotfix. Can you log the details in ITECH-301 so we have the temperature and location data?

Anthony - the cold-start issue is a hardware thermal sensor misread. The v4.2.1 patch adds a 30-second warm-up delay before the boot sequence. ETA for deployment is Jan 20. I know that's tight for your Feb 5 meeting but it should be rolled out by then.

Update on tablet situation for the brief - Dani says v4.2.1 hotfix targets Jan 20 deployment. I'm going to include the full incident timeline in the review. Four PagerDuty incidents for cold-start alone. Mike this needs to be a discussion item not just a footnote.
Agreed. Put it as its own section. Sandra's been asking about fleet tech reliability so this is directly relevant. Include the firmware version timeline and what each patch was supposed to fix.
I'll get you a tablet reliability report by Jan 28. Failure rates by route cold-start incident count and firmware patch history. I assigned ITECH-303 to myself.

Question for Rosa - the independent lot at exit 181 on I-65 (TS006 in Airtable) had another sketchy report from Ray Thornton last week. Guy approaching trucks at 2am asking for money. Third time in six months. Should we pull it from the approved list?

I saw Ray's report. Combined with the two previous incidents I think we should flag it as avoid-after-dark at minimum. I'll update PG-005 with the new language Anthony and I have been working on. The policy needs teeth - right now it's just a suggestion.
Anthony heads up your Atlanta load for next week got bumped to Thursday. New pickup time 0600 at the Fairburn DC. Kevin updated the dispatch board.
Copy Derek. That actually works better - gives me Wednesday to finish the atlas reconciliation. Kevin I'll need the updated BOL when it's ready.

Atlas reconciliation update - found 3 stops in the corporate atlas that aren't in our Airtable ratings and 2 stops in Airtable that got removed from the atlas last quarter. Need to sort out which dataset is the source of truth before the meeting.

The corporate atlas is supposed to be the master list but it only gets updated quarterly. Airtable has real-time driver ratings. I'd say present both and recommend we make Airtable the primary with quarterly atlas sync.
Good find. That's exactly the kind of operational gap Sandra wants to hear about. Include a recommendation slide on data source consolidation.
Ray Maria - reminder that the winter driving safety training module is due by Jan 31. Check Google Classroom. If your tablet won't load it use the desktop link I sent last week.
Also I need both of you to send me your corridor notes by Jan 20. Ray I need I-75 Chattanooga observations and Maria I need I-20 Atlanta side conditions. This is for the Feb 5 leadership review.
Reminder: Annual DOT physical compliance deadline is March 1 2027. Schedule your appointment through BambooHR. Contact Janet Lee with questions.
Unit 2022-T680-AH (Anthony's Kenworth) is due for PM service at 485000 miles. Currently at 483200. Schedule service during next home time window.
Copy Kevin. I'll be home Feb 3-5 for the meeting. Can we schedule the PM for Feb 6 at the Birmingham yard?
Another tablet question - the map tile cache keeps showing stale data on I-75 between Dalton and Chattanooga. Construction zone from last summer is still showing on the map. Is that a known issue?

Yeah that's INC-014 from December. The tile cache refresh job was failing silently. We fixed the job but some tablets still have the old tiles cached locally. Force close the maps app and clear cache from settings > apps > RidgeMap > storage.

Brief structure is coming together. Six sections: 1) Corridor incident trends 2) Truck stop atlas gaps 3) Independent lot safety flags 4) Fleet tablet reliability 5) Policy wiki updates needed 6) Recommendations. Sound right Mike?
That works. Add a section zero for executive summary - Sandra reads that first and decides how deep to go on each topic. Keep it to one page max.
Will do. Targeting Jan 28 for a draft to you for review. That gives us a week before the meeting for revisions.
